Hey, welcome to the Magnetic Email Marketing System and Toolkit livecast where you'll discover how to write emails that get delivered, get open, get read, build relationships with your customers, clients, patients, and subscribers, and put more money in your pocket. Now in this program we're going to talk about, and you're going to learn how you can easily create email campaigns that get results, including tele-seminar, webinar campaigns, referral campaigns, holiday campaigns, cash flow search campaigns, and more. After going through the system and toolkit, you'll be able to use email to automate a major, major part of your marketing, generate more repeat business and referrals, improve customer retention, and make revenue more predictable. All of course, which means an increase in your bottom line and more money in your pocket. So the Magnetic Email Marketing System and Toolkit consists of three main elements. First are the four one-hour segments you're about to watch and listen to. Now each one of these segments builds upon the teaching in the previous segment, so we really designed this in a cookbook format to make it easy for you. Just follow the email marketing recipe and you will be successful. So it's important that you go through each of the segments in order. The second element in the Magnetic Email Marketing System and Toolkit are the copyright-free email marketing documents that you can take and use to get almost immediate results with. Now we'll be referring to these documents throughout the program. In addition to the copyright-free marketing materials, there's also samples of successful email marketing documents that you can model. These come from our top, top members. We'll be talking about many of these documents during the program as well. Dan and I are going to share with you every secret we know about using email marketing that actually gets results. So welcome, Dan. Thank you, sir. Let's get started with this right away. I appreciate you fitting me in between all of those duties that you now have. That's great. We're starting off on a good note, aren't we? So let's talk about the power of email marketing but there's some things that the folks need to know about, things that are happening with email marketing which are making it more difficult than ever before which makes this type of program so important to them. Talk about some of those things. Well, it's clogged. The tubes of the internet are clogged. There's so much of it that you really have an effect not unlike what we had with direct mail in the 80s where literally everybody's mailbox was full and people became just unable to handle it, almost overwhelmed. So they, as Halbert said, everybody always starts their mail over a wastebasket but it really, everybody was going to the wastebasket and trying to get rid of as much as possible and you have that now. Everybody's sorting essentially with ignoring it or with a delete button but even in front of that you have deliverability issues, right? And so you have the issue of can the salmon swim upstream and actually even get there. If they get there, are they opened? And the open rate overall and for I would say most marketers, the open rate has been in decline and is in decline and will probably continue to be in decline. And a lot of people's sort of reaction to that is to throw more of it out there, right? So fewer people are opening something, we send them once a week, we'll send them once a day and fewer people open that so we'll send it every hour and fewer people open that so we'll send it every minute or we will send it 100,000, I will send a half a million, I will send 2 million and all that contributes to the whole problem. Again in a closed loop, there's a lack of exclusivity because it's cheap and easy, a lot of people do just bomb with no message to market match, no relevance, none of that which causes a reluctance to pay attention to any of it, right? And finally it's being hurt by drift to people paying attention to other forms of communication now in place of email, their tweets, their texts and mobile and all of that. There's a vulnerability to it as well which is my perpetual prediction that will probably be right before Gores about the rise of the seas from global warming and us all growing gills is that one way or another for commercial purposes there are taxes coming. So they're sort of your pile of negatives. In this session what we're going to do is talk about how to overcome those things because actually if there is taxing and there is charging for email that can actually be good if you know what you're doing. Well it has the same effect as a postage stamp in place, right? I mean in a sense it thins the herd, it does change economic dynamics just as the open rate problem changes the dynamics of the use of the media and it requires successful users to be smarter and smarter and more and more thoughtful about what it is that they are doing. So the first question I have a talk to you about is let's talk about capturing email addresses so we can actually market to them later on. Now we're going to talk about both online and offline methods for doing this. But first one of the keys is having what we call a lead generation magnet. That's actually a term you coined. How many years ago did you coin the term lead generation magnet? A hundred. A hundred years ago. A hundred years ago. You've been in the business for 40 years and you've coined it a hundred years ago. That's fantastic. So talk about exactly what a lead generation magnet is and what it's supposed to do. So when you want people to give you information about themselves, something as simple as an email address to a more complete package of information. So whether you want just email or you want email and full address, you want sort of climbing up the mountain of difficulty for most people. Email, email and full address, email and full address and phone numbers, email, full address, phone numbers and answer five questions so we can segment you. You have to give something in exchange. So there's a reluctance and a resistance to overcome that you best overcome by offering something that is magnetic, i.e. interesting, compelling, alarming, opportunity revealing in some way, shape or form a thing or access to a thing that I am so intrigued by that I want so much that I am willing to exchange whatever information you're asking me for knowing whether implicit or stated it includes permission for you to now pester me, badger me and pursue me. So when someone physically goes to a car dealership on Saturday to look at cars, their reluctance and resistance is not just about what the experience they are going to have with the salesperson in the hour they are in a dealership but am I going to be relentlessly pursued not thanks to the complete dysfunction and sloth of car salespeople that's a unrealistic fear. There will be no follow up, nobody will pursue you, you have nothing to worry about. When people give their email address they pretty much know, everybody knows it's free and I am going to be, my email inbox is now going to be clogged with crap from Dave, a lot of it forever once I give him this address. So the magnet is about exchange of value that is so weighted that I set aside my reluctance and resistance to that in order to get what it is that I want. A lot of people think who might be listening to this and watching this might be thinking wait a minute this is only for the information marketing guys, the online guys and that's not the case of the back runner show some examples of just a second, offline businesses need this too. I mean it's possible but it's rare so you want this information but in most cases it is more a precursor to getting other information and ascending a ladder of people's willingness to give you more and more information that you can use to effectively sell to them so it's like the first exchange. It's kind of like at old mixers you and I would exchange business cards, it's the first exchange. They often are because it's in many cases the least expensive thing you can do and it allows you to establish authority with the delivery of the lead generation magnet but it certainly doesn't have to be that. Well I don't care where do you want to go? You care. Right. So that will drag everybody to red Robin. The minute somebody is enrolled in a program is a member of something. Is a sees themselves as a participant in. They are infinitely more likely to open the email. Right. Correct. Than if they're just on a customer database and do not see themselves as in that relationship right. So they really accomplished several things with this program. One is they improve their open rate of email to their customers. Two they get them engaged in working towards the next reward which may in fact increase frequency of visit during a calendar year and in a business like theirs which is highly competitive and highly price competitive and like competitive businesses tend to be grouped together geographically. So literally there's oh that parking lot looks too busy I'll just go over here today. They're in their business. It's very important for them to get one more visit a quarter one more visit a year. So allows them to do that. Third it allows them to get data that they might not get otherwise birthdays for example wedding anniversary for example. Do you come here for business lunches or just for fun. Robert says is the toolkit applicable for network marketing and affiliate marketing. Then this goes to the my business is different. Yes it is. And in a sense no business is different. Right. And it's going to be fair. The network marketing industry and people in it as distributors tend not to understand what they the choice they've made. Being in a network marketing company you have made a choice to trade away margin for a lot of done for you services for a parent. So you for example have have opted out of product development product manufacturing product labeling legal liability insurance is a long list of responsibilities you have delegated upstream to the company that you're affiliated with in exchange you have traded away margin product margin and overall margin of the business even factoring in all of your overrides that does interfere with doing a lot of direct marketing things offline that we teach and that you might be able to do in other businesses. It does not preclude them but it requires deeper pockets and a longer period of time to recover the investment that many people are comfortable with. However online where we basically strip away 80 90 100 percent of the cost that that's not there right that barrier doesn't exist. So there any business every business is fundamentally on a level cost playing field and sure whatever we're talking about here applies there. So in the tool kit so if you take I'll give you a specific so we birthdays why the hell would you want somebody's birthday who is a prospect let's not take the retail side of network marketing let's take the recruiting side. Why would you want anybody's birthday. Well because New Year's right after the holidays birthdays particularly benchmark birthdays five and 10 year birthdays people are more reflective more frustrated more disturbed more questioning the nature of their life the amount of success they have or they don't have then they are at any other time during the year. Therefore if you have a bunch of accumulated leads and you time a recruiting pitch to arrive the week of your birthday you increase the likelihood of response because of the conversation you're having with yourself inside your own mind at that time. So it all applies right. Yeah exactly.
